title: JSON parses `false` but fails on `FALSE`
description:
### What happens?

JSON fails to understand uppercase `FALSE`.

### To Reproduce

```sql
select json_valid('{ "x" : "abc", "y" : FALSE, "z": 10} ') v;
/*
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ v     ‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î§
‚îÇ false ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
*/


select json_valid('{ "x" : "abc", "y" : false, "z": 10} ') v;

/*
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ v    ‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î§
‚îÇ true ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
*/
```

### OS:

Linux

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.2

### DuckDB Client:

Java

### Hardware:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_json (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data VARCHAR);
SELECT CASE WHEN json_valid(data) THEN 'true' ELSE 'false' END AS v FROM test_json WHERE id = 1;
SELECT CASE WHEN json_valid(data) THEN 'true' ELSE 'false' END AS v FROM test_json WHERE id = 2;
key_bug_features: JSON fails to recognize uppercase FALSE value
